name: Fresnedillas de la Oliva, Cebreros, Robledo de Chavela – circular route
name_original: Fresnedillas de la Oliva, Cebreros, Robledo de Chavela – ruta circular
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Fresnedillas De La Oliva are set within the Sierra Oeste of Madrid, offering varied terrain suitable for road cyclists. The region features dehesas, extensive grasslands, and oak forests, with granite formations adding to the landscape. Roads present a mix of flat sections and climbs, winding through villages and quiet secondary routes. The area's elevation ranges from approximately 700 meters to 972 meters at Peña Caballera, providing engaging ascents and descents. Best road cycling routes around Fresnedillas De La Oliva The most popular road cycling route is Fresnedillas de la Oliva, Cebreros, Robledo de Chavela – circular route, a 51.8 miles (83.3 km) trail that takes 4 hours 12 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through the region's diverse landscapes. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Robledo de Chavela, Valdemorillo, Navalagamella – circular route, a moderate 36.0 miles (58.0 km) path. This route offers a balanced ride through the area's wooded pastures and rural roads. Local road cyclists also love the Navalagamella, Fresnedillas de la Oliva, Robledo de Chavela – circular route, a 25.6 miles (41.2 km) trail leading through dehesas and quiet roads, often completed in about 2 hours 5 minutes. Road cycling around Fresnedillas De La Oliva is defined by dehesas, oak forests, and views of the Sierra de Guadarrama, with routes offering a mix of flat sections and climbs. The network provides options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Fresnedillas De La Oliva are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 3000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Fresnedillas De La Oliva's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Fresnedillas De La Oliva?
A: The terrain around Fresnedillas De La Oliva is varied and generally not overly steep, making it appealing for road cyclists. You'll encounter a mix of dehesas (wooded pastures), extensive grasslands, and oak forests. Routes often feature both flat sections for speed and engaging climbs, winding through picturesque villages and quiet secondary roads. The elevation ranges from approximately 700 meters to 972 meters at Peña Caballera, providing a good balance of ascents and descents.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Fresnedillas De La Oliva?
A: There are over 60 road cycling routes around Fresnedillas De La Oliva, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels. This includes 2 easy routes, 31 moderate routes, and 30 difficult routes.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes starting from Fresnedillas De La Oliva?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are circular. For example, the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e977180652/fresnedillas-de-la-oliva-cebreros-robledo-de-chavela-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fresnedillas de la Oliva, Cebreros, Robledo de Chavela – circular route&lt;/a&gt; is a popular option, covering 83.3 km with significant elevation gain. Another moderate choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e977179093/navalagamella-fresnedillas-de-la-oliva-robledo-de-chavela-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navalagamella, Fresnedillas de la Oliva, Robledo de Chavela – circular route&lt;/a&gt;, which is 41.2 km long.

Q: What natural features or viewpoints can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: Road cycling routes in the region offer stunning views of the Madrid mountains, including prominent peaks like El Yelmo in La Pedriza and La Almenara. From certain vantage points, such as the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/740607" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Seat of Philip II&lt;/a&gt;, you can enjoy panoramic views of the Guadarrama Valley and the Sierra de Navacerrada. The landscape is also characterized by unique granite formations known as 'bolos and berrocales', and you'll pass by ecologically valuable dehesas like Dehesa de Navalquejigo and Fuentelámparas.

Q: Are there any cultural or historical points of interest to explore near the cycling routes?
A: Absolutely. Fresnedillas de la Oliva offers several cultural and historical attractions. You can visit the Lunar Museum, which commemorates the Apollo 11 mission, or explore the 16th-century San Bartolomé Church. The town also features Costumbrista Graffiti on abandoned doors and facades, turning it into an open-air art gallery. Nearby, you can find the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4318799" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Royal Monastery of San Lorenzo de El Escorial&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Fresnedillas De La Oliva?
A: While not explicitly stated, the region's serene rural roads and varied landscapes suggest that spring and autumn offer ideal conditions for road cycling, with pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer mornings and late afternoons can also be enjoyable, avoiding the midday heat.

Q: What do other road cyclists think about the routes in Fresnedillas De La Oliva?
A: The road cycling routes in Fresnedillas De La Oliva are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 30 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, the mix of challenging climbs and flat sections, and the quiet rural roads that wind through charming villages.

Q: Are there any easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, there are 2 easy road cycling routes available around Fresnedillas De La Oliva. These routes are designed to be less demanding, offering a gentle introduction to the region's beautiful scenery on paved roads. While specific family-friendly routes are not detailed, the generally 'not very steep' topography makes the easier options suitable for a relaxed ride.

Q: How accessible is Fresnedillas De La Oliva by public transport for cyclists?
A: Fresnedillas de la Oliva is situated approximately 48-55 km from Madrid capital, making it an accessible escape. Public bus routes, such as the 645 and 627 from Madrid, serve the area. It's advisable to check the specific bus company's policy regarding bicycle transport before planning your trip, as rules can vary.

Q: Where can I find parking for my car if I want to cycle in Fresnedillas De La Oliva?
A: As a rural municipality, Fresnedillas de la Oliva typically offers public parking options within the town center or near key attractions. These spots are generally convenient for cyclists looking to start their routes from the village. Specific designated cycling parking areas are not detailed, but general public parking should be available.

Q: Are there any specific climbs or challenging sections for experienced road cyclists?
A: Yes, the region offers 30 difficult routes with significant elevation gain, providing a good challenge for experienced road cyclists. The varying altitudes, reaching up to 972 meters at Peña Caballera, ensure engaging ascents. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e977180652/fresnedillas-de-la-oliva-cebreros-robledo-de-chavela-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fresnedillas de la Oliva, Cebreros, Robledo de Chavela – circular route&lt;/a&gt; is classified as difficult due to its climbs and distance.

Q: Can I find places to eat or stay in Fresnedillas De La Oliva that are cyclist-friendly?
A: Fresnedillas de la Oliva, being a charming municipality, offers local restaurants and cafes where cyclists can refuel. While specific 'cyclist-friendly' establishments are not detailed, the welcoming nature of rural Spanish towns often means local businesses are accommodating to outdoor enthusiasts. Accommodation options are also available for those wishing to extend their stay.
tips: Navalagamella is known for its hiking trails and natural surroundings, perfect for outdoor activities. In addition, its historical and cultural heritage offers an enriching experience for visitors.

Incredible place full of antennas and radio telescopes to listen to space. The antennas impress, for their size and for the work they do. Check their official website to see if the museum is open or not.

In Fresnedillas de la Oliva there is a very original and fun route that runs through the streets of the town: the route is called ''Casas con Vida''. In the Fresnedillas tourist office you can find a brochure with the marked route - on the route you will see a total of 86 paintings and a large mural (painted by the artist Elena Parlange). Some of the most beautiful images are located in hidden places that must be discovered.
